overview: The OpenOffice Filter processes OpenOffice.org documents: **ODT** (text), **ODS** (spreadsheet), **ODP** (slides), **ODG** (graphics), and their corresponding template formats. These documents use the [OpenDocument format (ODF)](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenDocument). The filter handles the ZIP-based ODF package, extracting translatable content from multiple embedded XML files (`content.xml`, `meta.xml`, `style.xml`) as sub-documents.

extraction.extractNotes.help: Extracts the content of `<office:annotation>` elements (notes) as translatable text. When not set, notes are skipped entirely during extraction.
notes: **Warning:** The option to extract or not extract notes is reported as not working yet — notes may be extracted regardless of this setting.

extraction.extractMetadata.help: Extracts metadata fields from `meta.xml` for translation. The metadata file is processed as a separate sub-document within the ODF package.

extraction.extractReferences.help: Extracts the content of `<text:bookmark-ref>` elements. The content of these elements is only a **copy** of the referent — OpenOffice automatically overwrites it when the document is updated, so any translation done for these references will be lost on the next document update. However, extracting them can be useful to have the referenced text visible as part of the segment where it is inserted.
notes: **Warning:** The option to extract or not extract references is reported as not working yet — references may be extracted regardless of this setting.
Translated reference content will be **automatically overwritten** by OpenOffice when the document is updated.

extraction.encodeCharacterEntityReferenc.help: Controls whether character entity reference glyphs are encoded in the output.

limitations: Some **deleted text** may get extracted. Accept or reject all revision changes before processing the input document.
The options to extract or not extract **notes** and **references** are not working yet — they will be extracted regardless of the option settings.
Sequential **tabs may get reduced** to a single tab during extraction and merge round trip: space/tab elements are supported in output but incorrectly handled on input.
The target (output) encoding **must be set to UTF-8** when extracting documents to merge them back properly.
processingNotes: Output encoding is always **UTF-8** — any user-specified output encoding is ignored.
Line-breaks in the output are always set to **LF** (simple linefeed).

An ODF document is a ZIP file containing multiple XML files. The filter processes `content.xml`, `meta.xml`, and `style.xml` as **separate sub-documents**. In XLIFF output, a single ODT file produces three `<file>` elements — one for each embedded XML file. Typically only `content.xml` contains extracted text.
